fields.name: INCR_HDPL
fields.description: increment HDPL value Other: all other values allow a HDPL level increment.

fields.name: HDPL
fields.description: temporal isolation level This bitfield returns the current temporal isolation level.

fields.name: NEXTHDPL
fields.description: index to point to a higher HDPL than the current one Index to add to the current HDPL to point (through OBK-HDPL) to the next secure storage areas (OBK-HDPL = HDPL + NEXTHDPL). See for more details.

fields.name: AP_UNLOCK
fields.description: access port unlock Write 0xB4 to this bitfield to open the device access port.

fields.name: DBG_UNLOCK
fields.description: debug unlock when DBG_AUTH_HDPL is reached Write 0xB4 to this bitfield to open the debug when HDPL in SBS_HDPLSR equals to DBG_AUTH_HDPL in this register.

fields.name: DBG_AUTH_HDPL
fields.description: authenticated debug temporal isolation level Writing to this bitfield defines at which HDPL the authenticated debug opens. Note: Writing any other values is ignored. Reading any other value means the debug never opens.

fields.name: DBG_AUTH_SEC
fields.description: control debug opening secure/non-secure Write 0xB4 to this bitfield to open debug for secure and non-secure. Writing any other values only open non-secure.

fields.name: DBGCFG_LOCK
fields.description: debug configuration lock Reading this bitfield returns 0x6A if the bitfield value is different from 0xB4. 0xC3 is the recommended value to lock the debug configuration using this bitfield. Other: Writes to SBS_DBGCR ignored

fields.name: RSSCMD
fields.description: RSS command The application can use this bitfield to pass on a command to the RSS, executed at the next reset. When RSSCMD ≠ 0 and PRODUCT_STATE is in Open, then the system always boots on RSS whatever is the boot pin value.

fields.name: EPOCH_SEL
fields.description: select EPOCH value to be sent to the SAES 1x: EPOCH forced to zero (value used to retrieve PUF reference value at boot time)

registers.name: SECCFGR
registers.description: SBS security mode configuration control register
registers.fieldset_description: SBS security mode configuration control register

fields.name: SBSSEC
fields.description: SBS clock control, memory-erase status register and compensation cell register security enable

fields.name: CLASSBSEC
fields.description: ClassB security enable

fields.name: FPUSEC
fields.description: FPU security enable Note: This bit can only be written through privilege transaction.

fields.name: SDCE_SEC_EN
fields.description: control accessibility of SMPS_DIV_CLOCK _EN in SBS_PMCR

registers.name: PMCR
registers.description: SBS product mode and configuration register
registers.fieldset_description: SBS product mode and configuration register

fields.name: BOOSTEN
fields.description: booster enable Set this bit to reduce the total harmonic distortion of the analog switch when the processor supply is below 2.7 V. The booster can be activated to guaranty AC performance on analog switch when the supply is below 2.7 V. When the booster is activated, the analog switch performances are the same as with the full voltage range.

fields.name: BOOSTVDDSEL
fields.description: booster VDD selection Note: Booster must not be used when VDDA < 2.7 V, but VDD > 2.7 V (add current consumption). When both VDD < 2.7 V and VDDA < 2.7 V, booster is needed to get full AC performances from I/O analog switches.

fields.name: PB6_FMPLUS
fields.description: Fast-mode Plus command on PB(6)

fields.name: PB7_FMPLUS
fields.description: Fast-mode Plus command on PB(7)

fields.name: PB8_FMPLUS
fields.description: Fast-mode Plus command on PB(8)

fields.name: PB9_FMPLUS
fields.description: Fast-mode Plus command on PB(9)

fields.name: ETH_SEL_PHY
fields.description: Ethernet PHY interface selection Other: reserved

fields.name: FPU_IE
fields.description: FPU interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable the Cortex-M33 FPU interrupts FPU_IE[5]: inexact interrupt enable (interrupt disabled at reset) FPU_IE[4]: input abnormal interrupt enable FPU_IE[3]: overflow interrupt enable FPU_IE[2]: underflow interrupt enable FPU_IE[1]: divide-by-zero interrupt enable FPU_IE[0]: invalid operation interrupt enable

fields.name: MCLR
fields.description: erase after reset status This bit shows the status of the protection for SRAM2, BKPRAM, ICACHE, DCACHE, ICACHE and PKA. It is set by hardware and reset by software

fields.name: IPMEE
fields.description: end-of-erase status for ICACHE and PKA RAM This bit shows the status of the protection for ICACHE and PKA. It is set by hardware and reset by software.

registers.name: CCCSR
registers.description: SBS compensation cell for I/Os control and status register
registers.fieldset_description: SBS compensation cell for I/Os control and status register

fields.name: EN
fields.description: enable compensation cell for VDDIO power rail This bit enables the I/O compensation cell.

fields.name: CS
fields.description: code selection for VDDIO power rail (reset value set to 1) This bit selects the code to be applied for the I/O compensation cell.

fields.name: RDY
fields.description: VDDIO compensation cell ready flag This bit provides the status of the compensation cell.

registers.name: CCVALR
registers.description: SBS compensation cell for I/Os value register
registers.fieldset_description: SBS compensation cell for I/Os value register

fields.name: ANSRC1
fields.description: compensation value for the NMOS transistor This value is provided by the cell and must be interpreted by the processor to compensate the slew rate in the functional range.

fields.name: APSRC1
fields.description: compensation value for the PMOS transistor This value is provided by the cell and must be interpreted by the processor to compensate the slew rate in the functional range.

fields.name: ANSRC2
fields.description: Compensation value for the NMOS transistor This value is provided by the cell and must be interpreted by the processor to compensate the slew rate in the functional range.

fields.name: APSRC2
fields.description: compensation value for the PMOS transistor This value is provided by the cell and must be interpreted by the processor to compensate the slew rate in the functional range.

registers.name: CCSWCR
registers.description: SBS compensation cell for I/Os software code register
registers.fieldset_description: SBS compensation cell for I/Os software code register

fields.name: SW_ANSRC1
fields.description: NMOS compensation code for VDD power rails This bitfield is written by software to define an I/O compensation cell code for NMOS transistors of the VDD power rail. This code is applied to the I/O when CS1 is set in SBS_CCSR.

fields.name: SW_APSRC1
fields.description: PMOS compensation code for the VDD power rails This bitfield is written by software to define an I/O compensation cell code for PMOS transistors of the VDDIO power rail. This code is applied to the I/O when CS1 is set in SBS_CCSR.

fields.name: SW_ANSRC2
fields.description: NMOS compensation code for VDDIO power rails This bitfield is written by software to define an I/O compensation cell code for NMOS transistors of the VDD power rail. This code is applied to the I/O when CS2 is set in SBS_CCSR.

fields.name: SW_APSRC2
fields.description: PMOS compensation code for the VDDIO power rails This bitfield is written by software to define an I/O compensation cell code for PMOS transistors of the VDDIO power rail. This code is applied to the I/O when CS2 is set in SBS_CCSR.

fields.name: CLL
fields.description: core lockup lock This bit is set by software and cleared only by a system reset. It can be used to enable and lock the lockup (HardFault) output of Cortex-M33 with TIM1/8/15/16/17 break inputs.

fields.name: SEL
fields.description: SRAM ECC error lock This bit is set by software and cleared only by a system reset. It can be used to enable and lock the SRAM double ECC error signal with break input of TIM1/8/15/16/17.

fields.name: PVDL
fields.description: PVD lock This bit is set by software and cleared only by a system reset. It can be used to enable and lock the PVD connection with TIM1/8/15/16/17 break inputs.

fields.name: ECCL
fields.description: ECC lock This bit is set and cleared by software. It can be used to enable and lock the Flash memory double ECC error with break input of TIM1/8/15/6/17.

fields.name: LOCKNSVTOR
fields.description: VTOR_NS register lock This bit is set by software and cleared only by a system reset.

fields.name: LOCKNSMPU
fields.description: non-secure MPU register lock This bit is set by software and cleared only by a system reset. When set, this bit disables write access to non-secure MPU_CTRL_NS, MPU_RNR_NS and MPU_RBAR_NS registers.

fields.name: LOCKSVTAIRCR
fields.description: VTOR_S and AIRCR register lock This bit is set by software and cleared only by a system reset. When set, this bit disables write access to VTOR_S register, PRIS and BFHFNMINS bits in the AIRCR register.

fields.name: LOCKSMPU
fields.description: secure MPU registers lock This bit is set by software and cleared only by a system reset. When set, this bit disables write access to secure MPU_CTRL, MPU_RNR and MPU_RBAR registers.

fields.name: LOCKSAU
fields.description: SAU registers lock This bit is set by software and cleared only by a system reset. When set, this bit disables write access to SAU_CTRL, SAU_RNR, SAU_RBAR and SAU_RLAR registers.

registers.name: ECCNMIR
registers.description: SBS flift ECC NMI mask register
registers.fieldset_description: SBS flift ECC NMI mask register

fields.name: ECCNMI_MASK_EN
fields.description: NMI behavior setup when a double ECC error occurs on flitf data part

variants.name: Cell
variants.description: Code from the cell (available in the SBS_CCVR)

variants.name: B_0x6A
variants.description: recommended value to increment HDPL level by one

variants.name: B_0x0
variants.description: SBS_CFGR2 register accessible through secure or non-secure transaction

variants.name: B_0x1
variants.description: SBS_CFGR2 register only accessible through secure transaction
